message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #2 done, CI green, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #2 (CSS gaps) committed, pushed, and CI GREEN (2b5789a). 

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review and send consent or concerns. We need 9/9 consensus.

Changes: static/css/main.css — 7 items fixed:
1. .nav-logout — margin-inline-start: auto (right-aligned in header)
2. .nav-logout__button — styled as muted text link
3. .todo-add__group — spacing below input group
4. .todo-add__submit — fixed class name mismatch (was .todo-add__button)
5. .todo-add__error + :empty — danger color, small font, collapses when empty
6. .auth-form__message:empty — collapses empty error container
7. .auth-form__actions + .auth-form__cancel — flexbox layout, cancel as text-link

This addresses all of Steve Schoger's flagged CSS gaps.
</teammate-message>

content.content:
commit 2b5789af0ac53192c09fb1b197d7e51ee48a9666
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:25:24 2026 -0800

    Add missing CSS styles for nav-logout, todo-add errors, auth-form actions
    
    Addresses CSS gaps flagged during polish review:
    
    - .nav-logout / .nav-logout__button: position logout form right-aligned
      in header nav, style button as muted text link (not primary button)
    - .todo-add__group: spacing for the add-todo label+input group
    - .todo-add__submit: fix class mismatch (was .todo-add__button in CSS
      but .todo-add__submit in template)
    - .todo-add__error: danger color, small font, :empty collapse
    - .auth-form__message:empty: collapse empty error containers to remove gap
    - .auth-form__actions: flexbox row for save/cancel button pair
    - .auth-form__cancel: secondary text-link styling for cancel action
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 static/css/main.css | 62 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 1 file changed, 61 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

content.text: Good. Every CSS class in the new styles has a matching class in the templates. Now let me send my review.

input.content:
**Review of 2b5789a -- Kent Beck (TDD Coach)**

**I consent.**

**What changed:** 61 lines added to `static/css/main.css`, 1 class name corrected (`.todo-add__button` -> `.todo-add__submit`). Seven CSS gaps addressed.

**TDD perspective:**
- This is a CSS-only change. No behavior changed -- only visual presentation. Our integration tests verify HTML structure and content, not visual styling, so no new tests are expected here. This is appropriate.
- I verified that all existing tests still pass (CI green).
- I cross-checked every new CSS class against the templates: all classes in the CSS (`nav-logout`, `nav-logout__button`, `todo-add__group`, `todo-add__submit`, `todo-add__error`, `auth-form__actions`, `auth-form__cancel`, `auth-form__message:empty`) have corresponding class attributes in the Askama templates. No orphaned selectors.

**Design quality:**
- The `.todo-add__button` -> `.todo-add__submit` rename fixes a real class mismatch between CSS and template. Good catch -- the template used `todo-add__submit` but CSS had `todo-add__button`.
- `:empty` pseudo-class for collapsing empty error/message containers is the right pattern -- keeps the DOM always present for `aria-describedby` resolution while removing visual gaps when there's no error. This aligns with our a11y-first-in-DOM approach.
- `.nav-logout__button` and `.auth-form__cancel` both use the same "muted text link" visual pattern as `.button--text`. There's a small duplication opportunity here, but it's three similar blocks -- not worth abstracting yet. If a fourth appears, Tidy First.
- `margin-inline-start: auto` for `.nav-logout` is clean flexbox alignment. Logical property for RTL readiness.

Clean, complete fix addressing all flagged gaps. Good work.

- `margin-inline-start: auto` for `.nav-logout` is clean flexbox alignment. Logical property for RTL readiness.

Clean, complete fix addressing all flagged gaps. Good work."

content.text: Consent sent for Task #2. Lea's two tasks are now reviewed from my side. Standing by for the driver rotation to Carson Gross for Tasks #3-5 (HTMX enhancements).
